description: Trigger-accuracy and output-quality evals for the transfer-optimizer skill.

evals.name: transfers-window-open
evals.prompt: My team needs transfers this round — what should I do? I'm in group stage.
evals.expected_output: Full 12-step EV analysis with transfer table, captain pick, starting XI, bench, chip recommendation, and budget summary. Must include TRANSFER WINDOW: OPEN banner with deadline.

assertions.text: Calls get_game_rules to load stage budget and caps
Transfer table has OUT/IN columns with EV Gain and Budget Change
Validation checklist passes before output is presented

evals.name: transfers-window-closed
evals.prompt: Can I still make transfers? The round started — Brazil just kicked off.
evals.expected_output: TRANSFER WINDOW: CLOSED banner prominently at top. Steps 6–8 skipped. Next Round Watchlist shown instead of transfer table. Captain and formation analysis for next round.

assertions.text: Banners TRANSFER WINDOW: CLOSED at top of output
Does NOT recommend specific transfers (no OUT/IN table for this round)
Shows Next Round Watchlist of players to target
States when next window opens (after round's last match completes)

evals.name: captain-and-lineup
evals.prompt: Who should I captain and what lineup should I set for the quarter-finals?
evals.expected_output: Captain and vice-captain named with EV justification, fixture tier noted. Formation and bench stated. Differential flag shown if applicable (< 20% owned, EV > 75th pct). Uses predict_bracket_matchups for QF fixture tiers.

assertions.text: Captain named with EV value and fixture tier
Vice-captain named from different national team where possible
Uses predict_bracket_matchups (not group-stage hardcoded tiers) for QF fixtures
Differential flag shown if player owned by < 20% with EV above 75th percentile
